data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the council URL. Wait for the postcode input field with ID 'pPostcode'. Enter the user's postcode. Click the search button with class 'searchbox_submit'. Wait for the dropdown with ID 'uprn' to appear. Select the address using the provided UPRN value. Check the GDPR terms checkbox with ID 'gdprTerms'. Click the submit button. Wait for the results table with ID 'collection' to load. Parse the table rows to extract bin types and collection dates.
data.playwright_code:
async def scrape_bins(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto("https://www.calderdale.gov.uk/v2/residents/community-and-living/clean-streets/waste-and-recycling/collection-day")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#pPostcode")
    await page.fill("#pPostcode", postcode)
    await page.click(".searchbox_submit")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#uprn")
    await page.select_option("#uprn", value=uprn)
    await page.check("#gdprTerms")
    await page.click(".searchbox_submit")
    await page.wait_for_selector("#collection")
    rows = await page.query_selector_all("#collection tr")
    return rows
data.notes: The script requires a UPRN to select the specific property from the dropdown after the postcode search. Dates are parsed from strings like 'Tuesday 24 October 2023'.

data.api_description: The council uses a StatMap Aurora GIS system. The process involves requesting a session, fetching workflow task IDs, looking up coordinates for a postcode and matching a UPRN, and then executing spatial tasks (SaveState, DrillDown, FetchResultSet) to retrieve binary week/day offsets. Finally, it parses a separate bank holiday HTML page to adjust dates.
data.notes: The system uses a complex GIS-based multi-step API. Dates are calculated using a 14-day cycle from a known epoch (2025-01-13) and adjusted via offsets and a bank holiday lookup table scraped from the council website.

data.playwright_code:
async def scrape_bins(page, postcode):
    await page.goto(f'https://eppingforestdc.maps.arcgis.com/apps/instant/lookup/index.html?appid=bfca32b46e2a47cd9c0a84f2d8cdde17&find={postcode}')
    await page.wait_for_selector('.esri-widget--loader-container', state='hidden', timeout=20000)
    content_selector = '.esri-feature-content'
    await page.wait_for_selector(content_selector, state='visible', timeout=20000)
    elements = await page.query_selector_all(f'{content_selector} p')
    bins = []
    for el in elements:
        text = await el.inner_text()
        if ' collection day is ' in text:
            parts = text.split(' collection day is ')
            bins.append({'type': parts[0].strip(), 'collectionDate': parts[1].strip()})
    return bins
data.notes: The site uses an ArcGIS lookup application. The results are loaded dynamically after the find parameter is processed via the URL.

async def get_bins(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto('https://www.redbridge.gov.uk/bins-waste-and-recycling/collection-days/')
    search_input = await page.wait_for_selector('input.searchAddress')
    await search_input.fill(postcode)
    await page.click('button.searchAddressButton')
    address_link = await page.wait_for_selector(f'a[data-uprn="{uprn}"]')
    await address_link.click()
    await page.wait_for_selector('.your-collection-schedule-container')
    content = await page.content()
    return content

data.playwright_code:
async def get_bins(page, house_number, postcode):
    await page.goto('https://beta.npt.gov.uk/bins-and-recycling/bin-day-finder/')
    if await page.is_visible('#ccc-notify-accept'):
        await page.click('#ccc-notify-accept')
    await page.fill('#PostCode', postcode)
    await page.click("//button[@value='Find address']")
    await page.wait_for_selector('#Address')
    await page.select_option('#Address', label=f'*{house_number}*')
    await page.click("//input[@value='Find bin day']")
    await page.wait_for_selector('#contentInner')
    return await page.content()

data.playwright_code:
async def scrape_bins(page, postcode, uprn):
    await page.goto("https://my.portsmouth.gov.uk/en/AchieveForms/?form_uri=sandbox-publish://AF-Process-26e27e70-f771-47b1-a34d-af276075cede/AF-Stage-cd7cc291-2e59-42cc-8c3f-1f93e132a2c9/definition.json&redirectlink=%2F&cancelRedirectLink=%2F")
    iframe = page.frame_locator("#fillform-frame-1")
    await iframe.get_by_name("postcode_search").fill(postcode)
    await iframe.get_by_id("lookupAddress").click()
    await iframe.get_by_name("Choose_Address").select_option(value=uprn)
    await iframe.get_by_role("heading", name="next 10 collection dates").wait_for()
    
    bins = []
    containers = await iframe.locator("div[data-field-name^='html']").all()
    for container in containers:
        h4 = await container.locator("h4").text_content()
        if "next 10" in h4.lower():
            bin_type = h4.split(" - ")[0].strip()
            dates_text = await container.locator("p").first.inner_text()
            for line in dates_text.split('\n'):
                if line.strip():
                    bins.append({"type": bin_type, "date": line.strip()})
    return bins
data.notes: The site uses a Firmstep/AchieveForms implementation inside an iframe. Date strings are cleaned of special characters before parsing.

data.api_description: The script performs a POST request to an ASP.NET form using the UPRN in the 'txtRoadName' field and the postcode in 'txtPostCode'. It also requires session-based ASP.NET hidden fields like __VIEWSTATE and __EVENTVALIDATION.
data.notes: The code uses 'uprn' for the 'txtRoadName' field and 'postcode' for 'txtPostCode'. The bin types are identified as 'Pink Week' and 'Green Week'.

data.api_description: Takes a UPRN as a query parameter in the URL and returns HTML containing bin collection details.
data.notes: The script parses a specific div structure where 'content-left' contains the bin types (often split by ' and ') and 'content-right' contains the next collection date in a span with class 'nextCollectionDate'.

data.api_description: The API accepts a JSON payload with the UPRN and other fixed parameters. It returns a JSON object containing bin collection types and dates.
data.notes: Maps BinType IDs (2: Blue Bin, 6: Green Bin, 5: Black Bin, 3: Brown Bin) to friendly names. Filters for collections within the next 8 weeks.

data.api_description: Takes a UPRN and returns bin collection schedule in JSON format.
data.notes: The collection date contains a time component which is parsed using %d/%m/%Y %H:%M:%S. The service name is cleaned by removing the 'Collection Service' suffix.

data.playwright_steps: Go to the URL with the UPRN parameter. Extract the date from each <h3> tag and the bin types from the corresponding .binInfoContainer div children.
data.notes: The date format includes the full day of the week at the end without a space (e.g., '14 October 2023Saturday').

data.api_description: Takes the UPRN and queries a direct API that returns a JSON string (wrapped in extra quotes/backslashes).
data.notes: The API response requires cleaning by removing backslashes and trimming leading/trailing quotes before parsing as JSON. Year is inferred based on current system time.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the Tendring achieveservice page. Click 'close-cookie-message' if it appears. Click 'or, continue without an account' link. Switch to the iframe with ID 'fillform-frame-1'. Input the postcode into the field named 'postcode_search'. Wait for the 'selectAddress' dropdown to populate and select the option matching the UPRN. Extract collection data from the table with class 'wasteTable'.
data.notes: The process uses a multi-step form within an iframe. Waste types are normalized by removing parentheses. Dates are extracted from the 'Next collection' column using regex.

data.playwright_steps: Go to the council's bin collection page. Enter the postcode into the 'postcodeSearch' input. Click the 'govuk-button' to search. Select the address from the '#addressSelect' dropdown matching the house number. Wait for the 'Your next collections' header to appear. Scrape the bin types and dates from the 'p-2' divs.
data.notes: Uses get_next_occurrence_from_day_month logic to handle year transitions for dates formatted as '%A %d %B'.

data.api_description: A single GET request using the UPRN fetches the next collection dates for all bin types directly in JSON format.
data.notes: The API returns a status and a body containing collectionType and NextCollection fields.

data.api_description: The script visits a PHP endpoint that returns bin collection data in JSON format, however, it is protected by Cloudflare and requires a browser to resolve.
data.playwright_steps:
1. Navigate to the API URL with the UPRN.
2. Wait for Cloudflare verification to complete (document state complete).
3. Extract the JSON content from the body of the page.
4. Parse the 'type' and 'nextDate' fields from the JSON.
data.notes: The site uses Cloudflare protection so standard HTTP requests will likely fail. The URL returns a JSON array directly inside the body.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the page, click the 'Continue' button, switch to the 'fillform-frame-1' iframe, input the postcode into 'postcode_search', select the property from the 'chooseAddress' dropdown using the UPRN, click 'Next', and extract the bin collection schedule from the table in the 'subCollectionCalendar' field.
data.notes: Uses a Granicus AchieveForms platform. Requires switching to an iframe and handling UPRN-based address selection.

data.calendar_description: The council uses a schedule-based calculation based on a collection day (passed as paon) and a round identifier (Round A or Round B passed as postcode). It calculates alternating fortnightly collections for Green/Grey bins and Blue/Brown bins, with weekly collections for Food bins.
data.notes: The logic relies on calculating offsets from specific reference dates: 2025-11-17 and 2025-11-24. 'paon' must be a weekday string (e.g., Monday) and 'postcode' must be either 'Round A' or 'Round B'.

data.api_description: Submits a multipart/form-data POST request with the postcode and UPRN to a multi-page form and scrapes the resulting HTML table for collection dates.
data.notes: The form uses a multipart/form-data payload with specific fields including __token, page (128), and specific field IDs for postcode and UPRN. The year is not provided in the response and must be inferred from current date context.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the Torbay recycling page. Click the bin collection link which opens a new window. In the new window, input the postcode into the field with ID 'FF1168-text'. Click search. Select the address from the dropdown list with ID 'FF1168-list' by matching the UPRN in the option values. Click the submit button with ID 'submit-button'. Once the results load, extract the service type and date from the container with ID 'resiCollectionDetails'.
data.notes: The site uses a multi-window approach where the actual lookup form is hosted on a separate portal. The UPRN is prefixed with 'U' and suffixed with '|' in the dropdown values.

data.api_description: A SOAP request is sent to the web service with a hardcoded password 'wax01653' and the user's UPRN. The response contains an XML-wrapped HTML fragment with bin collection details.
data.notes: The SOAP response contains an inner text result which is actually HTML. Dates are extracted from strings like 'Mon 11 Oct' and year is appended dynamically. SOAP Body contains a call to getRoundCalendarForUPRN.

data.api_description: First, a GET request is made to the authentication endpoint to retrieve a session ID (sid). This sid is then passed as a query parameter in a POST request to the runLookup endpoint, along with the UPRN in the JSON body to retrieve bin collection details.
data.notes: The lookup requires an 'id' parameter (6314720683f30) and a session ID from the authapi call. The UPRN is provided in the formValues.Property.siteReference.value path of the POST body.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the URL. Enter the postcode into the input with id 'postcode' and press Enter. Select the house number (PAON) from the 'housenn' dropdown. Click the submit button with alt text 'View your waste collection days'. Wait for the 'Future Collection Dates' header. Parse the resulting table rows for bin image alt text and dates.
data.notes: The script uses Selenium to interact with a multi-step form. Dates in the source HTML include ordinal indicators (e.g., 1st, 2nd) which are stripped before parsing. Note that the year is hardcoded to 2024 in the original source, which may require dynamic year adjustment logic.

data.api_description: First, the script calls a JSONP endpoint using the UPRN to retrieve bin week info (roundday_residual) and recycling codes. It then constructs a URL to fetch an HTML calendar page based on the retrieved bin week schedule.
data.notes: The script handles custom month abbreviations like 'Sept'. It combines bi-weekly black bag collections from the HTML table with calculated weekly recycling collections based on the weekday found in the initial API response.

data.api_description: The script sends a GET request to the council's bin zone URL with a specific UPRN stored in a cookie named SVBINZONE (formatted as VALE%3AUPRN%40{uprn}). It also includes a SOVA_TAG parameter set to VALE.
data.notes: The UPRN must be prefixed with 'VALE%3AUPRN%40' inside the 'SVBINZONE' cookie. The scraper looks for bin types in 'bintxt' and dates in the subsequent 'binextra' div. Dates are parsed using day and month names and then assigned a year based on current system time.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the provided URL (custom_component_show_url_field). Locate the 'Bin collections' heading. Find sibling divs with class 'c-content-section_body'. Iterate through the bin types (tablet:l-col-fb-4 u-mt-10). Extract the bin type from 'u-mb-4'. Extract 'Next collection' text and additional dates from the list 'u-mt-4'.
data.notes: The URL requires a specific format like 'https://www.wakefield.gov.uk/where-i-live?a=[ADDRESS]'. The original scraper uses BeautifulSoup to parse the driver's page source.

data.api_description: The script queries an initial endpoint with the UPRN to retrieve a list of links for each bin type. It then iterates through these individual bin schedule links to extract dates from the tables on each page.
data.notes: The process involves an initial scrape of the main schedule page followed by sub-requests for each specific bin round's calendar page. Dates are then parsed from the table rows.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the URL. Switch to the iframe with ID 'fillform-frame-1'. Wait for the input 'postcode_search' to be clickable. Enter the postcode. Click the 'lookupPostcode' button. Wait for the 'select2-choice' dropdown and click it. Enter house number (paon) into the 'select2-input' field and press Enter. Click the 'confirmSearchUPRN' button. Wait for the 'Next Collections' header. Extract bin types from 'h5' tags and dates from 'p b' tags.
data.notes: The script uses an iframe 'fillform-frame-1'. The date extraction logic appends the current year and handles the rollover to the next year using a helper function.

data.api_description: A single GET request to the council property search page using the UPRN returns HTML containing bin collection details.
data.notes: The code extracts bin types from 'h4.collection-heading' and then looks for siblings with class 'collections' containing 'strong' tags for 'Next:' or 'Previous:' collections and their associated dates.

data.api_description: Takes a UPRN and returns a JSON object containing bin schedules with bin types and start dates.
data.notes: The API returns a JSON structure with a 'schedule' array. Each item has 'Name' for the bin type and 'ScheduledStart' for the date.

data.api_description: The script scrapes the bin collection dates directly from a property-specific calendar page using the UPRN.
data.notes: The script extracts bin types from image alt tags and dates from paragraph elements within the waste-dates div. It splits strings to isolate the date portion.

data.api_description: Initial GET call to SESSION_URL retrieves a session token (sid) from auth-session. This sid is then passed as a query parameter to the runLookup API endpoint along with the UPRN in a JSON body to fetch collection data. The response contains HTML in the dispHTML field which is then parsed.
data.notes: The HTML content returned in the JSON response at data['integration']['transformed']['rows_data']['0']['dispHTML'] contains list items with bin types in h3 tags and dates in strong tags.

data.api_description: First, it gets the homepage to extract a session track ID. Then it POSTs the postcode to a search endpoint. Finally, it uses the 'pindex' (passed as house_number/PAON) and the track ID to fetch the collection results page.
data.notes: The 'pindex' parameter is required for the final URL and is mapped from the user's PAON/house_number input. The system uses Whitespace software which relies on a 'Track' session ID in the URL.

data.api_description: Sends a POST request to the WordPress admin-ajax endpoint with an action parameter and the UPRN to retrieve a JSON object containing collection dates for refuse, recycling, and garden waste.
data.notes: The request requires the action parameter 'wealden_get_collections_for_uprn' in the POST body alongside the uprn.

data.api_description: Initial GET to fetch a __token from the form page, followed by a POST request containing the token, postcode, and uprn to retrieve the collection table.
data.notes: The form uses dynamic input names for postcode and uprn (q9f451fe0ca70775687eeedd1e54b359e55f7c10c_0_0 and q9f451fe0ca70775687eeedd1e54b359e55f7c10c_1_0 respectively). These may change if the form version is updated.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the bin day URL. Enter the postcode into the input field with ID 'FINDYOURBINDAYS3WEEKLY_ADDRESSLOOKUPPOSTCODE'. Click the search button with ID 'FINDYOURBINDAYS3WEEKLY_ADDRESSLOOKUPSEARCH'. From the resulting dropdown with ID 'FINDYOURBINDAYS3WEEKLY_ADDRESSLOOKUPADDRESS', select the option containing the house number/PAON. Wait for the collection dates div with ID 'FINDYOURBINDAYS3WEEKLY_RUBBISHRECYCLEFOODDATE' to appear. Scrape the dates from the specific divs for rubbish (black), recycling (green), and food (purple).
data.notes: The code handles 'Today' as a special case for the date and adjusts for the year rollover if the current month is December and the collected date is in January.

data.api_description: Takes a UPRN as a query parameter and returns a page containing bin collection schedules in HTML format.
data.notes: The script parses 'span.date-string' for the collection date and 'div.round-name' for the bin type. It maps specific keywords like 'bag'/'blue' to 'Blue', 'caddy'/'brown' to 'Brown', and 'non-recyclable' to 'Black'.

data.api_description: The script first performs a GET request with the postcode to find a list of addresses in an ASP.NET table. It then finds the row matching the user's UPRN and extracts the __EVENTARGUMENT from the href. A POST request is then made to the same URL with the VIEWSTATE and EVENTTARGET to fetch the specific bin collection dates for that address.
data.notes: Uses ASP.NET __VIEWSTATE and __EVENTVALIDATION. The bin dates are specifically found in span elements like ctl00_MainContent_lbNextDomRoundZones, ctl00_MainContent_lbNextRecRoundZones, and ctl00_MainContent_lbNextGardenRoundZones.

data.api_description: First, the script performs a location lookup using a concatenated address string (house number and postcode) to retrieve X, Y coordinates and a location ID. Second, it calls a 'getpage' endpoint with these coordinates and ID to retrieve a JavaScript payload containing HTML-formatted bin collection dates.
data.notes: The dates are returned in a JavaScript string payload as HTML. The year must be inferred based on the current month to handle year-rollover in December. The HTML is extracted from a 'document.getElementById("DR1").innerHTML' assignment and then unescaped from unicode.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to URL, click the 'no' feedback banner, enter postcode into #WLBINCOLLECTION_PAGE1_ADDRESSLOOKUPPOSTCODE, click search button #WLBINCOLLECTION_PAGE1_ADDRESSLOOKUPSEARCH, select address matching house_number from #WLBINCOLLECTION_PAGE1_ADDRESSLOOKUPADDRESS, wait for .bin-collections table to appear, extract bin types and dates from .bin-collect divs.
data.notes: The date parser needs to remove ordinal indicators (st, nd, rd, th) before parsing with %A, %B %d %Y.

data.api_description: The script sends a GET request to the council website using the UPRN to retrieve the waste collection schedule page which contains the bin collection dates.
data.notes: The parser iterates through each waste-collection__month div, extracts the month from the h3 tag and individual collection days from li.waste-collection__day items. It handles year rollover by checking if the current month is late in the year (after September) and the collection month is early (before April).

data.api_description: Single API call using the UPRN in the URL path to retrieve bin collection items.
data.notes: The API returns an object containing a list called 'collectionItems' which includes the waste type and the date.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the waste collection enquiry page. Enter the full address (house number and postcode) into the search input. Wait for the address dropdown and select the first address result. Click the next button. Scrape the table for bin container types and collection dates.
data.notes: Handles 'Today' and 'Tomorrow' date edge cases by converting them to the current or next day. Year is inferred from the current date.

data.api_description: The API returns HTML content containing bin collection information associated with the provided UPRN. The script extracts bin types and their corresponding dates from a specific panel data div.
data.notes: The parser looks for a div with class 'atPanelData' that is a sibling or child of a parent containing an 'h4' with the text 'Bin collection days'. It iterates through the text content in pairs (bin name and date).

data.api_description: Initial GET to the MyNeighbourhood page to retrieve ASP.NET state variables. A first POST performs a postcode search to load address options. A second POST uses the UPRN (prefixed with 'UPRN' and zero-padded to 12 digits) to retrieve the final page containing bin collection dates.
data.notes: The UPRN must be 0-padded to 12 characters and prefixed with 'UPRN' in the form data. Dates are extracted from 'div.dateWrap-next' and cleaned of ordinal suffixes (st, nd, rd, th) before parsing.

data.api_description: The council uses an API endpoint that returns HTML calendar data for a given month, year, postcode, and UPRN. The script iterates through the current and next two months to fetch collection dates.
data.notes: The UPRN is zero-padded to 12 characters. The API returns an HTML calendar where collection events are nested within day cells. Multiple collection types per day are split by ' and '.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the URL. Input the postcode into #postcodeSearch. Click the search button (.govuk-button.mt-4). Wait for the address dropdown and select the option containing the paon. Wait for the results container to load (div.ant-row.justify-content-between). Scrape the H3 for bin type and the .fw-bold div for the date text.
data.notes: The date parser handles year-wrapping logic for year-end collections. It assumes the current year unless the current month is late (Nov/Dec) and the collection month is early (Jan/Feb).

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the URL composed of the base RBWM forms URL and the provided UPRN. Locate the div with class 'widget-bin-collections'. Within this div, iterate through each table body row to extract the bin type from the first cell and the collection date from the second cell.
data.notes: The script uses the UPRN directly in the query string of the RBWM forms URL. It relies on a BeautifulSoup-style parsing of the table rows within the specific widget-bin-collections div.

data.api_description: The script loops through three waste types: 'Non recycleable (green bin)', 'Garden waste (brown bin)', and 'Paper and packaging (grey bin)'. It sends a GET request for each type, passing a street and suburb extracted from the uprn parameter.
data.notes: The UPRN input is expected to be a comma-separated string containing street and suburb (e.g., 'StreetName,SuburbName'). The script iterates over a predefined list of waste types to fetch specific dates for each.

data.api_description: Initial GET to root to extract a dynamic base link, followed by a POST to the address lookup endpoint (mop.php) with the PAON and postcode. The resulting page contains a link to the property-specific collection calendar.
data.notes: The site uses a WhiteSpace service. The code extracts pairs of dates and bin types from a list. It specifically reformats 'Batteries-small electricals-textiles' to use slashes instead of hyphens.

data.playwright_steps:

data.api_description: Calls a specific URL containing the postcode and UPRN which returns a landing page with bin collection information.

data.api_description: Submits the UPRN in a form-encoded POST request to retrieve a table of bin collection schedules.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to the site; Click 'Start now'; Click 'continue without an account'; Switch to the iframe with id 'fillform-frame-1'; Enter the postcode in 'LocationSearch'; Wait for results to load; Select the address using the UPRN value in the 'ChooseAddress' dropdown; Scrape the collection data from the resulting table with id 'wcbc_collection_details'.

data.playwright_steps: Navigate to URL. Handle cookie consent if visible. Enter postcode in input 'alAddrtxt'. Click 'alAddrbtn'. Wait for dropdown 'alAddrsel'. Select address by UPRN value. Click 'btnSubmit'. Wait for results table containing 'Upcoming collections'. Extract bin types and dates from table rows.

data.api_description: Retrieves an HTML snippet containing collection dates for a specific UPRN via a GET request.
data.notes: The API returns HTML. Bin types are extracted from h3 tags and dates from the content div. Year logic is applied manually based on the current date since the response only contains day/month.

data.calendar_description: The council uses a bi-weekly rotation based on a fixed start date. Black/Grey Rubbish Bin starts on 2024-11-25 and repeats every 14 days. Green Recycling Bin starts on 2024-11-18 and repeats every 14 days. An offset is applied based on the day of the week provided in the input (paon).
data.notes: The input 'paon' is used as the day of the week (e.g., 'Monday', 'Tuesday') to calculate the specific collection day relative to the base rotation dates.

data.api_description: Calls the York waste API directly using the UPRN to retrieve bin collection services and dates.
data.notes: The API returns a 'services' list containing 'service' names and 'nextCollection' ISO timestamps.
